From Courtroom
to Growth Engine

A non-linear path that's become a competitive edge — combining legal rigour, financial acumen, and product-led growth.

I started my career as a litigation attorney in South Africa, where I learned to build airtight arguments, navigate complexity under pressure, and advocate for outcomes with evidence — skills that turned out to be remarkably transferable to product management.

After moving into banking at First National Bank, I discovered a passion for scaling products that genuinely change how people interact with financial systems — taking an estate planning tool from 2,000 customers a year to 8 million within 24 hours.

"I believe the best growth leaders are as comfortable in a data warehouse as they are in a strategy session — and I've spent a decade making sure I'm both."

Today, as Senior Growth Product Manager at Numa, I own the full direct booking funnel — running continuous experimentation across web, mobile, and app to drive measurable revenue. My next step is into a Growth Lead or Head of Growth role where I can lead the function, not just the experiments.

FinTechMVPPayments

Delivering an MVP for Payment Integration

How I led the new Payments team at ToolTime to deliver MVP payment rails, enabling digital invoicing for craftsman businesses through an Adyen integration.

The Client: Empowering Craft Businesses

ToolTime is a leading ERP system tailored for craftsman businesses — connecting craftspeople providing services with the end customers seeking their expertise. The platform had accumulated over €60 Million worth of invoice payments per month, all processed manually. The mission was to change that.

The Mission: Interim Senior Growth Product Manager

I was brought in to stand up a brand-new Payments team (3 engineers, 1 designer, supported by a UX researcher and Product Lead) and bridge the gap while a permanent PM was recruited. The goal was to initiate and drive the integration with Adyen — the payments infrastructure provider — and get the team into a high-functioning delivery rhythm from day one.

Notably, a permanent PM signed their offer on my first day, which compressed the engagement. Rather than slow down, I pivoted to ensuring a seamless handover while still delivering meaningful progress toward the MVP.

The Initiative: Payments Rail MVP

😣 The Pain Point

The existing manual payment process created friction for every stakeholder — craftspeople, end customers, and the platform itself. Beyond UX, there was a clear revenue opportunity being left on the table: a take-rate on €60M+ in monthly invoice payments that was entirely uncaptured.

🚝 The Solution: Payments Rail

A Payments Rail — an automated, digital payment layer integrated into the platform — designed to support multiple payment methods including Apple Pay and card payments, enabling seamless money movement between craftspeople and their customers.

Journey to MVP

Challenges on Arrival

I joined to find minimal onboarding, the CPO on vacation, and no clear documentation of current progress or the path forward. The first task was to create clarity — for the team, for stakeholders, and for the incoming permanent PM.

Strategic Onboarding

I built user flows from scratch to understand the platform's operations and the craftsman industry. I immersed myself in Adyen's documentation to build a clear picture of the integration's technical and product requirements — becoming the team's primary domain expert within the first week.

User Story Map for Clarity

I crafted a detailed User Story Map covering all activities, tasks, and project details. This became the team's single source of truth — making it immediately clear what was done, what was in flight, and what remained for the MVP.

End-to-End User Story Map for the Payment Integration with Adyen

Building Blocks to MVP

  1. Leveraging Past Research: Reviewed existing user research to understand features already under development.
  2. Discovery Validation: Identified gaps and ran a targeted mini-discovery to validate findings from a session conducted two years prior.
  3. User Flow for MVP: Created a comprehensive user flow for the MVP that addressed missing components and ensured product coherence.

Simplified User Journey Map for Stakeholder Updates — reflecting what was in/out of scope and current progress

Feature Workshops for Alignment

Given time constraints, I ran focused workshops for each MVP feature — working through technical requirements, building CPO buy-in, and aligning the team. The CPO had a strong preference to be involved in product decisions; these workshops became the mechanism for channelling that energy productively.

Refunds Flow Workshop with C-Level and the Product Lead

Detailed Handover for Continuity

Knowing my role was temporary, I invested heavily in the handover — building a comprehensive blueprint covering the project overview, the rationale behind every decision, open questions, and future opportunities surfaced during the engagement.

Handover Blueprint for the newly onboarded Product Manager of the Payments Team

Delivered Outcomes

Efficient Payment Infrastructure: Successfully introduced a Payments Rail, streamlining and automating transactions across all parties.
Operational Excellence: Created clarity from ambiguity — a detailed User Story Map and structured onboarding replaced the previous undocumented state.
User-Centric Innovation: Validated features through workshops and targeted research, ensuring the MVP was grounded in real user needs.
Seamless Handover: Delivered a detailed handover package that gave the permanent PM a running start and secured continuity of the programme.

Originally published on getproductpeople.com

SaaSProduct DiscoveryContinuous Discovery

Integrating Continuous Discovery into PDFTools / Smallpdf

How I embedded continuous discovery, outcome-focused roadmapping, and a new team operating model into the PDF Web Viewer SDK team — increasing Key Result completion and shifting the team toward an experimentation mindset.

The Client: PDFTools / Smallpdf

Pdftools is a company specialising in software solutions for PDF and PDF/A documents, founded in 2002. Their tools support creating, processing, viewing, and archiving PDFs for developers and businesses. Pdftools was acquired by Smallpdf in March 2022.

Smallpdf, founded in Switzerland in 2013, offers a user-friendly platform with over 20 PDF tools that has served over a billion users in 195 countries — covering conversion, editing, compression, e-signatures, and OCR.

The Mission: Interim Technical Product Manager

I joined PDFTools / Smallpdf to strengthen the PDF Web Viewer SDK product and define the direction for product development by considering internal and external customers, end users, competitors, and market trends.

🧑‍💻 The Product

The PDF Web Viewer provides PDF viewing and annotation functionality for browser-based applications. It is a developer-facing SDK — developers integrate it into their own products (CRMs, documentation apps, etc.) to add PDF functionality.

🧑‍🤝‍🧑 The Team

3 developers (1 FE + 2 BE). After I joined, the Engineering Manager moved to support other teams — I took on scrum event facilitation as a result. No dedicated UX designer or researcher; I created low-fidelity Figma designs and drew on UX research consultancy as needed. A Group PM had been supporting multiple teams — I came in as dedicated PM for the PDF Web Viewer team.

Fast Onboarding: First Month Deliverables

  1. Created user flows for developers (our customers) and end users, plus a competitor flow to identify gaps and inspiration.
  2. Prepared a gap analysis comparing our product's features against internal customer needs, aligned with the internal customer on findings.
  3. Reviewed customer support tickets to map external customer needs, then built a matrix analysis to focus on the intersection of internal needs and external requests.
  4. Ran a workshop with the dev team to define Q4 2022 OKRs — each with problem statement, validation approach, strategy contribution, increments, risks, and dependencies.

OKR Planning Workshop

In parallel, I aligned ways of working, scrum cadences, and team structures in an internal kick-off workshop, and delivered a quarterly first-draft roadmap for 2023 — including user and business problems, proposed solutions, and dependencies — aligned across all product teams.

Quarterly Planning Workshop with all Product Teams

Integrating Continuous Discovery

When I joined, the team had no regular customer conversations to test assumptions or validate ideas. I changed that systematically.

  1. Initiated monthly meetings with prospective and existing clients to understand needs, share product updates, and clarify release scopes.
  2. Built a Product Trio with a developer from the team and drew on the UX research function for consultancy.
  3. Conducted interviews with 3 existing customers plus 2 candidates with SDK product experience found via User Testing.
  4. Synthesised feedback into interview snapshots, feeding an Opportunity Solution Tree (OST) to identify the highest-value opportunities at the intersection of customer needs, product outcomes, and market trends.
  5. Ran a focused workshop to explore the chosen opportunity — generating solutions and surfacing assumptions collaboratively with the team.
  6. Prepared a Hotjar survey for customers and website visitors to validate key assumptions about the focus opportunity.

Ideation Session with the Dev Team

Involving the Team in Decision-Making

The company had started using OKRs before I arrived, but the framework wasn't embedded in the team's daily work. I made OKRs a team practice, not just a management tool.

  1. Ran strategy sessions with the dev team evaluating which problems to prioritise next — based on interview results, impact, and effort.
  2. Defined objectives and key results for prioritised initiatives collaboratively with the team.
  3. Established weekly OKR updates and monthly completion tracking, and used end-of-quarter reviews to inform the next cycle.
  4. Analysed B2B customer requests and company outcomes, ran competitor analysis, and built a critical feature list evaluated with the team on effort and dependencies — resulting in an outcome-oriented roadmap on ProductBoard, aligned with all stakeholders.

OKR Workshop with the Whole Team

Leading the Transition from Scrum to Scrumban

The team was struggling with Scrum's rigidity — complex interdependent tasks and urgent B2B client needs were regularly disrupting sprint planning and execution.

  1. Mapped the existing Scrum flow and conducted a thorough review to identify bottlenecks.
  2. Interviewed all developers to gather first-hand insights on friction points and improvement ideas.
  3. Designed a Scrumban flow that blended Scrum's structure with Kanban's flexibility.
  4. Replaced standard planning meetings with Replenishment meetings focused on Kanban board prioritisation.
  5. Implemented WIP limits for "In Progress" and "In Review" columns to reduce bottlenecks.
  6. Refined the approach collaboratively with the team to ensure buy-in and address concerns before rollout.

The process to switch to Scrumban

Delivered Outcomes

Experimentation mindset: A cultural shift toward testing and validating assumptions — a significant stride in product development and customer engagement.
Outcome-focused roadmap: Shifted from feature-based to problem-and-outcome oriented roadmapping, enhancing strategic alignment.
Ownership & accountability: Fostered a culture of team ownership with a stronger focus on achieving key results.
Adaptive workflow: Established a more efficient Scrumban workflow, allowing the team to manage ongoing tasks and respond swiftly to client needs.
Smooth handover: Fully onboarded the new Senior PM. Engagement was extended once before concluding.

Originally published on getproductpeople.com

FinTechBankingComplianceProduct Delivery

Balancing Compliance with User Value at N26

How I supported the Customer Identity segment at N26 — a European banking unicorn with 8 million customers — in discovering and delivering compliance-driven initiatives while preserving user and business value.

The Client: N26

N26 is a fintech pioneer founded in 2013 and one of Europe's most prominent digital banks, serving 8 million customers across Europe and the US. The company has raised close to US$1.8 billion from world-class investors and operates at the forefront of the digital banking revolution.

The Mission: Interim Product Manager, Customer Identity

I acted as support to the Product Lead overseeing two teams within the Customer Identity segment — primarily dealing with the onboarding flow, market compliance, and market-specific IBAN migrations. I was brought in to fast-track discovery and delivery across 3 delivery teams while a permanent PM was being hired, running topics as if I were an internal product manager. A notable constraint: no direct access to company or usage data — solved through close collaboration with internal staff members.

The engagement ran July 2023 – February 2024 (extended from the original December 2023 close).

The Main Quest: Balancing Risk, Compliance, and Value

Our product scope involved solving compliance challenges that were actively capping N26's growth numbers, while finding ways to balance regulatory requirements with business and user goals. Initiatives spanned: French IBAN migration, SIRET and IE compliance, Customer Document Re-verification flows, and TaxID collection.

Initiative 1 — French IBAN Migration

The Problem

All N26 customers in France had non-French IBANs, causing friction — most critically, many French employers require a French IBAN to process salary payments.

What I Did

The bulk of delivery had been completed before I joined. I oversaw usability testing, produced a prioritised list of required changes, and worked with the delivery team to address the highest-priority items — resulting in a successful rollout of the French IBAN migration to the entire French market.

Initiative 2 — Reducing Customer Support Tickets for Document Verification

The Problem

A strategic goal was reducing Customer Support costs. One of the top contributors: fraud and false-positive fraud cases within the Customer Document Verification module.

What I Did

I met with Customer Support and Operations Managers to map end-to-end flows. I identified the top-priority fixes and delivered a detailed, prioritised requirements document for the responsible PM. The changes were accompanied by a business case that I helped prepare and got signed off by the segment Director.

Process unpacking and prioritisation workshops with Customer Support and Operations

Initiative 3 — TaxID Collection: Balancing Compliance with Conversion

The Problem

The existing TaxID collection process was overly risk-averse, creating friction in the onboarding funnel and driving direct loss of customers and revenue.

What I Did

I met with tax, compliance, and regulatory teams to understand the non-negotiables, ran competitor research across European banks, and gathered C-Level input on revenue loss and growth targets. I documented all findings in a Confluence page with a video walkthrough of the top 2 recommended solutions — signed off by the Managing Director.

Structure and Table of Contents for the TaxID Proposal Document

Initiative 4 — SIRET & IE Compliance for the French Market

The Problem

French business customers were being onboarded without SIRET and IE compliance in place — requiring a costly manual correction post-onboarding, resulting in the loss of 46% of those customers.

What I Did

I worked with French operations, compliance, and regulatory teams to understand the law's scope and bare minimum requirements. I unpacked the current technical solution with the engineering team, balanced compliance needs against technical feasibility, and created a detailed Confluence page covering the problem and solutions. The prioritised solution was implemented with minimum technical time and cost investment.

Executive Summary within the Confluence Page detailing the SIRET/IE problem and solutions

Side Missions

Proof of Address Standardisation (Germany → France): A standalone module built for Germany needed adapting for the French market. I mapped French compliance requirements and provided the delivery team with minimal-investment requirements to achieve French KYC compliance.

Post-reorg team kick-off: Following a restructuring, I facilitated kick-off workshops for newly assembled teams — aligning on scope, ways of working, and goals.

Delivered Outcomes

French IBAN Migration: Successfully tested and rolled out to the entire French market.
TaxID Collection: Risk-balanced approach to TaxID collection — Director sign-off secured, protecting conversion while maintaining compliance.
SIRET & IE Compliance: French business onboarding brought into full compliance with minimal delivery investment.
Document Verification: Fraud and false-positive issues documented and prioritised, reducing Customer Support cost exposure.
Extended engagement: Initial contract July–December 2023 extended through February 2024.

Originally published on getproductpeople.com

SaaSB2BProduct OpsDAU Growth

Automating SaaS Inventory Visibility & Growing DAUs at Sastrify

How I led product delivery at Sastrify's Solar Flares team — shipping Partner API integrations with Google Workspace, Xero, and Netsuite, launching User Roles, and embedding Continuous Discovery — increasing Daily Active Users on the platform.

The Client: Sastrify

Sastrify is a B2B SaaS procurement platform that helps companies track their SaaS tool stack, discover the right tools, and save money on licenses and subscriptions. Named one of Sifted's best European start-ups to work for, Sastrify is on a mission to provide the right software, lightning-fast, at fair prices.

How Sastrify Works

The Mission: Interim Product Manager

I joined Sastrify's Solar Flares Product & Tech team as Interim PM on parental cover, embedded within a team of Front and Back-end Engineers, an Engineering Manager, and a UI/UX Designer. The engagement spanned 6 months, bridging until a permanent PM was hired — who I also helped onboard.

The mandate: automate visibility into B2B customers' SaaS tool inventories and increase Average Daily Active Users on the platform.

Delivery: Partner API Integrations

Sastrify's Partner API Integrations

I delivered integrations with Google Workspace, Xero, and Netsuite, enabling B2B customers to automatically discover their company's SaaS tools with a single click — eliminating a manual onboarding process that previously took several weeks. This set the foundation for further accounting software integrations and automated spend updates from those integrations.

Delivery: User Roles on Platform

Sastrify's User Roles

I created and shipped User Roles — giving B2B customers complete control over how their teams contribute to the company tool stack and what level of visibility each role has (financial data, documents, contracts). User Roles directly increased Sastrify's Daily Active Users by enabling more team members to participate in the platform, while reinforcing the trust Sastrify had built with customers.

Continuous Discovery

Continuous Discovery at Sastrify

I maintained a regular cadence of customer interviews and testing sessions, keeping Opportunity Solution Trees (OSTs) current and ensuring delivery was grounded in real customer outcomes. The OSTs formed a central part of the handover to the incoming PM.

Onboarding the New PM

I delivered a comprehensive handover spanning 24+ pages — covering team and stakeholder introductions, ways of working, OKRs, ongoing topics, released features, and a full library of essential links. Paired with several deep-dive sessions with the new PM, this ensured they could hit the ground running on complex, interconnected initiatives from day one.

Delivered Outcomes

Partner API integrations shipped: Google Workspace, Xero, and Netsuite — automating SaaS inventory discovery.
User Roles launched — increasing Daily Active Users and giving B2B customers granular access control.
Continuous Discovery embedded — up-to-date OSTs focused on driving team and company outcomes.
OKR definition and planning completed collaboratively with the team.
New PM successfully onboarded within weeks via structured handover documentation and sessions.
